content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a Tiny Macro Lisp system that compiles to COR24 assembly. Phase 1: lexer and parser. Phase 2: evaluator with core special forms. Phase 3: compiler targeting COR24 assembly. Phase 4: integration with tc24r and cor24-rs toolchain.

=== STEPS ===
  [!] 001-lexer [production]: Implement the Lisp lexer: tokenize parentheses, symbols, numbers, strings, an...
  [x] 002-research-and-plan [production]: Read research, study COR24 toolchain, create architecture/prd/design/plan/stats docs
  [x] 003-scaffold [production]: Set up C source structure based on architecture
  [ ] 003-003-scaffold [legacy]: Set up C source structure: create src/tml.h (Value type, tags, macros, forwar...
  [x] 004-003-scaffold-cor24 [legacy]: Redo scaffold for COR24 target. CC=tc24r, run with cor24-run --run. No libc h...
  [ ] 004-lexer [production]: Implement tokenizer for Lisp syntax
  [x] 005-reader [production]: Implement S-expression reader
  [ ] 005-005-reader [legacy]: Implement S-expression reader in src/read.h. Parse from char* string input. H...
  [x] 006-eval-core [production]: Implement eval with core special forms
  [x] 007-gc [production]: Implement mark-sweep garbage collector
  [x] 008-compiler [production]: Compile to COR24 assembly
  [ ] 009-integration [production]: Wire to tc24r and cor24-rs toolchain <-- YOU ARE HERE

=== CURRENT STEP: 009-integration [production] ===
Status: pending
Description: Wire to tc24r and cor24-rs toolchain

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

content.content:
1→TC24R = tc24r
     2→COR24_RUN = cor24-run
     3→SRC = src/main.c
     4→ASM = build/tml24c.s
     5→
     6→all: $(ASM)
     7→
     8→$(ASM): $(SRC) src/tml.h src/io.h src/heap.h src/symbol.h src/print.h src/read.h src/eval.h src/gc.h src/compile.h
     9→	@mkdir -p build
    10→	$(TC24R) $(SRC) -o $(ASM)
    11→
    12→run: $(ASM)
    13→	$(COR24_RUN) --run $(ASM) --speed 500000
    14→
    15→clean:
    16→	rm -rf build
    17→

file.content:
TC24R = tc24r
COR24_RUN = cor24-run
SRC = src/main.c
ASM = build/tml24c.s

all: $(ASM)

$(ASM): $(SRC) src/tml.h src/io.h src/heap.h src/symbol.h src/print.h src/read.h src/eval.h src/gc.h src/compile.h
	@mkdir -p build
	$(TC24R) $(SRC) -o $(ASM)

run: $(ASM)
	$(COR24_RUN) --run $(ASM) --speed 500000

clean:
	rm -rf build

input.new_string:
run: $(ASM)
	$(COR24_RUN) --run $(ASM) --speed 500000

test: $(ASM)
	@echo "Running tml24c tests..."
	@$(COR24_RUN) --run $(ASM) --speed 500000 -n 2000000 2>&1 | \
		grep -E "^(scaffold|reader|eval|gc|compile) ok$$" | sort > build/test-results.txt
	@expected=5; got=$$(wc -l < build/test-results.txt | tr -d ' '); \
	if [ "$$got" -eq "$$expected" ]; then \
		echo "All $$expected test suites passed."; \
		cat build/test-results.txt; \
	else \
		echo "FAIL: $$got/$$expected test suites passed:"; \
		cat build/test-results.txt; \
		exit 1; \
	fi

clean:
	rm -rf build

test: $(ASM)
	@echo "Running tml24c tests..."
	@$(COR24_RUN) --run $(ASM) --speed 500000 -n 2000000 2>&1 | \
		grep -E "^(scaffold|reader|eval|gc|compile) ok$$" | sort > build/test-results.txt
	@expected=5; got=$$(wc -l < build/test-results.txt | tr -d ' '); \

toolUseResult.originalFile:
TC24R = tc24r
COR24_RUN = cor24-run
SRC = src/main.c
ASM = build/tml24c.s

all: $(ASM)

$(ASM): $(SRC) src/tml.h src/io.h src/heap.h src/symbol.h src/print.h src/read.h src/eval.h src/gc.h src/compile.h
	@mkdir -p build
	$(TC24R) $(SRC) -o $(ASM)

run: $(ASM)
	$(COR24_RUN) --run $(ASM) --speed 500000

clean:
	rm -rf build

Add make test target for automated CI-friendly validation

COR24 emulator with a 2M instruction limit to avoid the infinite REPL
loop, then verifies all suites report "ok".
